title: Kaukenia cuneifolia
taxonomy: Kaukenia cuneifolia is classified within the flowering plant family Fabaceae, also known as legumes. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and its ability to form nodules that fix nitrogen from the air. The genus Kaukenia is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which contains many other legume species. Kaukenia cuneifolia is native to New Caledonia, an island region in the Pacific Ocean. The specific classification of Kaukenia cuneifolia reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Kaukenia cuneifolia, a plant native to Australia, has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and roots contain compounds that have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and infections.
